description_long: This is a large, modern commercial airplane with a streamlined fuselage and long, swept-back wings. The wings are mounted low on the body and feature noticeable upward-angled winglets at the tips. Two sizable turbofan engines are suspended beneath the wings, one on each side, indicating a twin-engine configuration. The tail section consists of a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, forming a classic tail design. The exterior is predominantly white with dark gray wings, and the surface appears smooth and glossy, typical of commercial airliners. The cockpit is positioned at the forward end of the fuselage, with a multi-windowed, enclosed design.
description: A large, white commercial airplane with swept-back low wings, twin underwing turbofan engines, and a conventional tail. The surface is smooth and glossy, with a forward-mounted cockpit and upward-angled winglets.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pointed nose, forward cockpit windows, and symmetrical low-mounted wings with upward-angled winglets and visible twin engines.
description_view_1: Left side profile highlighting the elongated fuselage, low-mounted swept wing, single visible engine, and the conventional tail with a vertical stabilizer.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section, both engines, and the full span of the swept-back wings with winglets.
description_view_3: Right side profile similar to the left, showing the opposite engine, wing, and tail configuration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, long swept-back wings with upward-angled winglets and slight dihedral angle.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear (not visibly deployed)
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with multiple windows
